name: Yvain
primary_sources: Yvain, the Knight of the Lion by Chrétien de Troyes
Owain, or the Lady of the Fountain from the Mabinogion

visual_attributes.morphology: Human male knight in medieval armor
visual_attributes.texture: metallic and smooth for armor, furry for the lion
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Average human male height, approximately 6 feet tall
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Knight in armor with a lion by his side

domain_specific_visual.lion_position: Standing or walking beside the knight

relationships.description: Yvain's wife whom he seeks to win back
A loyal lion that accompanies Yvain on his adventures
The knight Yvain defeats to avenge his cousin
Laudine's servant who helps Yvain win back his wife

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the ideals of chivalry and knightly virtue
primary_sources: Yvain, the Knight of the Lion by Chrétien de Troyes

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic medieval knight without a lion
NOT a modern knight with contemporary armor
NOT a character from non-Arthurian legends
